description: Replace ffmpeg-based segmentation with AI-first event-aware clip discovery, add activity tags to events, and generalize the Vision prompt system.

userStories.title: Add ActivityTag enum and activityTags field to Event schema
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I want the Event model to store multi-select activity tags (sports, party, play, speech) so that downstream AI scoring can use per-event context to shape what counts as interesting.
acceptanceCriteria: An ActivityTag enum is added to schema.prisma with values: SPORTS, PARTY, PLAY, SPEECH
The Event model gains an `activityTags` field of type `ActivityTag[]` (native PostgreSQL array via `String[]` in Prisma)
A migration is generated via `prisma migrate dev` and committed
Existing events without activityTags default to an empty array without migration errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-28. ActivityTag enum added with values SPORTS, PARTY, PLAY, SPEECH. Event model gains activityTags String[] @default([]). DB synced via prisma db push (shadow db had prior migration conflict, so db push was used instead of migrate dev). Prisma client regenerated. Commit: 260c409

userStories.title: Define ActivityTag TypeScript type and ACTIVITY_CONTEXT map in shared constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a canonical TypeScript ActivityTag type and the activity-to-context lookup map in a shared module so that all pipeline code uses the same definitions without duplication.
acceptanceCriteria: A new file `src/lib/activity-tags.ts` exports `type ActivityTag = 'sports' | 'party' | 'play' | 'speech'`
The file exports `const ACTIVITY_CONTEXT: Record<ActivityTag, string>` with the exact context strings specified in the design (sports, party, play, speech)
The file exports `buildVisionPrompt(activityTags: ActivityTag[]): string` that assembles BASE_PROMPT + activity context blocks + SCORING_INSTRUCTIONS in that order
`buildVisionPrompt([])` still returns a valid prompt consisting of BASE_PROMPT + SCORING_INSTRUCTIONS with no activity blocks in between
The Prisma `ActivityTag` enum values map 1:1 to the TypeScript union members (case-insensitive normalization in wiring code)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-28. New file src/lib/activity-tags.ts exports ActivityTag type, ACTIVITY_CONTEXT map, ACTIVITY_TAG_VALUES allowlist, and buildVisionPrompt(). All criteria verified. Commit: 0997821

userStories.title: Update analyzeTemporalInterestingness to accept activityTags instead of sport
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I want analyzeTemporalInterestingness to accept ActivityTag[] and use buildVisionPrompt to construct its prompt at runtime, so that Vision API calls are event-aware rather than hardcoded around youth sports.
acceptanceCriteria: The function signature of `analyzeTemporalInterestingness` in `src/lib/ai-interestingness.ts` changes: the `sport` and `eventName` string parameters are replaced by `activityTags: ActivityTag[]`
The hardcoded youth-sports prompt (lines ~7-85 of ai-interestingness.ts) is replaced by a call to `buildVisionPrompt(activityTags)` imported from `src/lib/activity-tags.ts`
When `activityTags` is empty, the Vision call uses BASE_PROMPT + SCORING_INSTRUCTIONS only — no activity context blocks
The window scoring and JSON response parsing logic remains unchanged (only the prompt changes)
All existing tests in `__tests__/ai-interestingness.test.ts` are updated to pass `activityTags` instead of `sport`/`eventName`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The exact current prompt string spans roughly lines 7-85 of ai-interestingness.ts. The replacement must preserve the 3-frame window group structure and the 0-100 scoring dimensions.

userStories.title: Update analyzeQuoteQuality to increase sensitivity when speech tag is present
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I want analyzeQuoteQuality to accept activityTags and boost its scoring threshold when `speech` is present, so that spoken content in speech-centric events (keynotes, coaching sessions) gets properly surfaced as child CLIP assets.
acceptanceCriteria: The function signature of `analyzeQuoteQuality` in `src/lib/ai-interestingness.ts` changes: the `sport` and `eventName` string parameters are replaced by `activityTags: ActivityTag[]`

When `activityTags` includes `'speech'`, the quote quality scoring threshold is lowered by 15 points (from the current cutoff — currently ~60 based on existing score-clip.ts filtering) so more quotes are surfaced
When `'speech'` is NOT in `activityTags`, quote quality scoring behaves identically to today
The function still returns `QuoteScore[]` with the same shape (text, startTime, endTime, speakerLabel, quoteQualityScore, reason)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The exact current quote score threshold in score-clip.ts lines ~79-95 should be verified during implementation. The 15-point reduction is relative to that threshold.

userStories.title: Thread activityTags from Event through INGEST_CLIP and SCORE_CLIP job payloads
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I want activityTags to be read from the Event record and included in job payloads for INGEST_CLIP and SCORE_CLIP, so that every AI scoring call has access to per-event context.
acceptanceCriteria: INGEST_CLIP handler in `src/lib/handlers/ingest-clip.ts` reads `activityTags` from the Event record (via prisma.event.findUnique) before enqueuing SCORE_CLIP jobs
The INGEST_CLIP job payload (constructed in `src/lib/queues.ts` or the caller) includes `activityTags: string[]` alongside existing fields like `eventId`, `sport`, `eventName`
SCORE_CLIP handler in `src/lib/handlers/score-clip.ts` reads `activityTags` from its job payload and passes it to both `analyzeTemporalInterestingness` and `analyzeQuoteQuality`
The SCORE_CLIP handler passes `activityTags` to `analyzeAndSegment` in `src/lib/scene-detection-service.ts` (for future Track B use)

Existing `sport` and `eventName` fields on the job payload are preserved for backward compatibility during the transition — new code reads `activityTags` first and falls back to inferring tags from `sport`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The fallback inference from `sport` to `activityTags: ['sports']` is a transitional measure and should be logged with a deprecation warning.

userStories.title: Update event creation API to accept and store activityTags
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select activity tags (sports, party, play, speech) when creating or editing an event so that AI scoring is tailored to my event type.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/events` in `src/app/api/events/route.ts` accepts an optional `activityTags: string[]` field validated against the allowlist ['sports', 'party', 'play', 'speech']
`PATCH /api/events/[id]` in `src/app/api/events/[id]/route.ts` accepts an optional `activityTags: string[]` field with the same validation
Invalid or unknown tag values return a 400 error with message `"Invalid activity tags: <comma-separated list>"`
Prisma create/update calls include `activityTags` in the data payload
Events created without `activityTags` default to an empty array
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add activity tag multi-select to the event creation and edit modals
userStories.description: As a user, I want a tag selector in the create/edit event modal on the Dashboard page so that I can pick which activity types describe my event.
acceptanceCriteria: The create event modal in `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` (lines ~450-524) gains a new field `Activity Type` with four toggleable pill buttons: Sports, Party, Play, Speech
The edit event modal (lines ~527-598) gains the same activity tag selector, pre-populated with the event's current tags
At least one tag must be selected; the form shows validation error if none selected on submit attempt
Selected tags are sent to the API as `activityTags: string[]` in the POST/PATCH request body
The tags display in the event detail page (`src/app/events/[id]/page.tsx`) as colored pill badges
The `createForm` state (line 85 of dashboard/page.tsx) includes `activityTags: string[]` initialized to `['sports']` as default
The edit flow pre-populates `editForm.activityTags` from the fetched event data
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The current `createForm` state is defined on line 85 of dashboard/page.tsx with fields { name, sport, city, eventDate, description }. Tailwind classes for pills can follow the existing accent/color pattern used throughout the UI. The event detail page at src/app/events/[id]/page.tsx will need minor addition to render activity tags.

userStories.title: Implement computeAudioEnergyProfile in ffmpeg-utils.ts
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I want a function that uses ffmpeg volumedetect to produce a timeline of audio energy levels across an entire source video, so that candidate windows for AI scoring can be pre-filtered by audio activity without requiring STT.
acceptanceCriteria: `computeAudioEnergyProfile(videoPath: string)` is added to `src/lib/ffmpeg-utils.ts`, returning `Promise<AudioEnergyProfile>`
`AudioEnergyProfile` is typed as `{ segments: Array<{ startTime: number; endTime: number; meanDb: number; maxDb: number }>; duration: number }`
The function uses `spawnLimitedFfmpeg` with the `volumedetect` filter to compute mean_volume and max_volume per 1-second windows across the full video
The `nice` parameter is set to 15 and `timeoutMs` to 300_000 to match existing ffmpeg-utils conventions
Stderr output is parsed to extract `mean_volume` and `max_volume` dB values per segment, converting to a structured array
If ffmpeg fails or produces no usable output, the function returns an empty profile with `segments: []` and the video duration from ffprobe
Memory usage stays within OOM-safe bounds: streaming stderr parsing with 64 KB buffer, no full-buffer accumulation beyond what's needed for single-line parsing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: volumedetect does not natively produce per-second windows. Implement by segmenting the audio stream with `asegment` and then applying `astats` per segment, OR by running volumedetect on the full stream and post-processing the single mean/max values as a single-segment profile for MVP. The acceptance criteria allow a simplified initial implementation where the full-video mean/max is treated as one segment — refine to per-second windows in a follow-up if needed.

userStories.title: Implement identifyCandidateWindows to merge motion density and audio energy into ranked time windows
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I want a function that merges motion density scoring and audio energy profiles into ranked candidate time windows sorted by interest likelihood, so that only promising windows are sent to the Vision API.
acceptanceCriteria: `identifyCandidateWindows(motionProfile: MotionDensityResult, audioProfile: AudioEnergyProfile, duration: number)` is added to `src/lib/video-segmentation.ts` (or a new `src/lib/candidate-windows.ts`), returning `Promise<CandidateWindow[]>`
`CandidateWindow` is typed as `{ startTime: number; endTime: number; motionScore: number; audioScore: number; combinedScore: number }`

Motion density is computed by running ffmpeg with `select='gt(scene,0.1)'` at low threshold (retained as one signal) to get motion change timestamps, then scoring 1-second buckets by density of motion changes
Audio score per window is derived from `computeAudioEnergyProfile` — windows overlapping high-energy audio segments get higher scores
Combined score = motionScore * 0.4 + audioScore * 0.6 (audio-weighted since speech/celebration events have low motion but high value)
Windows shorter than 2 seconds are merged with adjacent windows
Windows longer than 30 seconds are split at the lowest-confidence point
Maximum 30 candidate windows returned, ranked by combinedScore descending
If both motion and audio profiles are empty, returns a single window spanning the full video duration (fallback to uniform sampling)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor analyzeAndSegment to use AI-first candidate windows on source video
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I want analyzeAndSegment in scene-detection-service.ts to run AI temporal interestingness on the source video using candidate windows (not on post-segmentation child clips), so that AI scoring guides segmentation rather than following it.
acceptanceCriteria: `analyzeAndSegment` in `src/lib/scene-detection-service.ts` is refactored: instead of calling `segmentVideo` → create child clips → then run `analyzeTemporalInterestingness` on children, the new flow is:
1. Call `computeAudioEnergyProfile(sourceVideoPath)` to get audio energy timeline
2. Call ffmpeg motion detection at low threshold (scene=0.1) to get motion density profile
3. Call `identifyCandidateWindows(motionProfile, audioProfile, duration)` to get ranked candidate windows
4. Extract frames at the midpoints of each candidate window (one frame per window, up to 30 windows)
5. Call `analyzeTemporalInterestingness(sourceAssetId, sourceVideoPath, candidateWindowsAsFrames, activityTags)` to score each window
6. Apply boundary refinement: snap to I-frames, add 0.5s pre-roll / 1.0s post-roll buffers, merge windows within 2s of each other, enforce min 3s / max 30s clip lengths
7. High-scoring windows (combinedScore ≥ threshold) become child CLIP Asset records with `parentAssetId`, `startTimeMs`, `endTimeMs`, `durationSeconds`, `motionLevel`, `dominantMode`
The existing `segmentVideo` function and `detectScenes` continue to exist as utility functions (not removed) but are no longer the primary segmentation gate
The `analyzeAndSegment` function signature gains `activityTags: ActivityTag[]` as a parameter
Existing tests in `__tests__/clips-child-assets.test.ts` continue to pass after refactor
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The current `analyzeAndSegment` signature in scene-detection-service.ts is `(sourceAssetId: string, sourceVideoPath: string, eventId: string, sport?: string, eventName?: string)`. It orchestrates the full segmentation → child clip creation → SCORE_CLIP enqueue pipeline. The refactor must preserve child Asset creation with the same schema fields (parentAssetId, startTimeMs, endTimeMs, durationSeconds, motionLevel, dominantMode) and the same SCORE_CLIP enqueue pattern.

userStories.title: Update INGEST_CLIP handler to pass activityTags into analyzeAndSegment and ensure quote analysis runs for speech events
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I want the INGEST_CLIP handler to pass activityTags into the refactored analyzeAndSegment and to trigger analyzeQuoteQuality when the speech tag is present, so that speech-centric events get transcript-based clip discovery during ingest.
acceptanceCriteria: The INGEST_CLIP handler in `src/lib/handlers/ingest-clip.ts` passes `activityTags` from its job payload into `analyzeAndSegment` (already accepted by US-010)
When `activityTags` includes `'speech'`, the handler runs `analyzeQuoteQuality` on the source video's transcript during INGEST_CLIP (not only during SCORE_CLIP)
Quote clips discovered by `analyzeQuoteQuality` are created as child CLIP Assets with `dominantMode: 'SPEECH'` and the transcript excerpt stored in their `transcriptWordsJson` field
Quote clips are enqueued for SCORE_CLIP alongside motion/audio-discovered child clips
When `'speech'` is NOT in `activityTags`, `analyzeQuoteQuality` is still called during SCORE_CLIP (existing behavior) but NOT during INGEST_CLIP — no duplicate API calls

The existing behavior of running `analyzeQuoteQuality` in SCORE_CLIP (src/lib/handlers/score-clip.ts, lines ~79-95) is updated to check if quote clips already exist from INGEST_CLIP and skip redundant re-analysis
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Speech quote analysis during INGEST_CLIP is a new pathway — the handler currently only runs transcription during ingest (for transcriptWordsJson on the parent Asset). Quote analysis during SCORE_CLIP currently uses the stored transcript, so duplicating the analysis at ingest time requires careful deduplication logic to avoid double-counting.

userStories.title: Retain ffmpeg scene detection as one signal source (not primary gate) and ensure child CLIP asset creation is unchanged
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I want ffmpeg scene detection retained as a motion-density signal input to identifyCandidateWindows rather than the primary segmentation gate, and for child CLIP Asset creation to follow the exact same schema pattern used today.
acceptanceCriteria: The `detectScenes` function in `src/lib/video-segmentation.ts` is retained and called at a low threshold (0.1) inside `identifyCandidateWindows` or `analyzeAndSegment` to produce motion density data — NOT as the gate that determines clip boundaries
The `segmentVideo` function is not deleted; it is marked with a JSDoc `@deprecated` comment noting it has been superseded by AI-first segmentation

Child CLIP Asset records created by `analyzeAndSegment` have all the same fields as today: `type: 'CLIP'`, `parentAssetId`, `startTimeMs`, `endTimeMs`, `durationSeconds`, `motionLevel` ('LOW'/'MEDIUM'/'HIGH'), `dominantMode` ('ACTION'/'SPEECH'/'MIXED'/'MONTAGE')
Child CLIP creation uses `prisma.asset.create` with the same field set used in ingest-clip.ts today (no new or missing fields)
The existing test in `__tests__/clips-child-assets.test.ts` that verifies child asset fields passes without modification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story is tightly coupled with US-010 and should be implemented as part of the same changeset. It's split here for traceability of the specific requirement to retain scene detection as a signal source.
